expected_properties: valid_out remains low when no new parallel word is presented after a completed serialization
dout does not change unexpectedly during idle periods
additional_cases.goal: Verify that the module does not assert valid_out or change dout when the input d is held constant after a full 4‑bit conversion.
notes: Uses the same input d value that was previously serialized; no new load event is triggered.
preconditions: A full serialization of a previous 4‑bit word has completed (valid_out has been observed high for one cycle).
The input d is kept stable (no change) for at least four additional clock cycles.
stimulus_intent: Hold d constant after a completed conversion and monitor outputs.
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs for at least four clock cycles after the last valid_out assertion.

expected_properties: Outputs do not become X when the input d contains X or Z bits
valid_out does not assert spuriously for illegal input patterns
additional_cases.goal: Check robustness of the design when the parallel input contains unknown or high‑impedance values.
notes: The contract does not forbid X/Z on d; this case ensures conservative handling.
preconditions: Drive d with at least one X or Z bit for a single clock cycle.
stimulus_intent: Apply an X/Z pattern to the parallel input and observe module response.
timing_assumptions: Capture output immediately after the clock edge that samples the X/Z input.

expected_properties: valid_out asserts high for exactly one cycle per loaded 4‑bit word
dout outputs the four bits of d in MSB‑to‑LSB order over four consecutive cycles
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Confirm that a single legal parallel word is serialized correctly and that valid_out signals the start of serialization.
notes: Assumes the module asserts valid_out when the first bit of the new word is presented.
stimulus_intent: Provide a representative 4‑bit pattern on d and observe the resulting serial stream.
timing_assumptions: Wait for at least five clock cycles after driving d to capture the full serialization and the next idle period.
